channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: How to write a holiday rom-com for TV, according to the experts
item.description: Have you ever watched a holiday rom-com on Hallmark Channel or Lifetime — or any of the other many networks and streamers that now air them — and thought, “I could write that”? It's harder than you may think — but just as fun.
item.dc:creator: ALICIA RANCILIO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden's commutation in 'kids for cash' scandal angers some Pennsylvania families
item.description: A judge who helped orchestrate one of the worst judicial scandals in U.S.history — a scheme to send children to for-profit jails in exchange for kickbacks — was among the 1,500 people whose sentences were commuted by President Joe Biden this week.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL RUBINKAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers demand answers about mysterious drones
item.description: Federal investigators say they've gotten reports about drones flying over military bases and even near President-elect Donald Trump's golf course in New Jersey, but they don't know who's flying them.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9

item.title: Dick Vitale says he's cancer-free after 4 bouts with disease in just over 3 years
item.description: Vitale had surgery in the summer to remove cancerous lymph nodes from his neck. He was previously treated for melanoma and lymphoma, and had radiation treatments last year for vocal cord cancer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 3 men suing Diddy for rape 'have chance to get power back': Lawyer
item.description: The lawsuits involve incidents taking place from 2019 to 2022. The men are all identified as John Doe.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: O’Malley: Calling Social Security a 'legal Ponzi scheme' is bull
item.description: "Social Security is not on a path for bankruptcy or insolvency," O'Malley said during a Thursday appearance on "The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Back trouble and brain fog bothered suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing, his posts show
item.description: After Luigi Mangione made the difficult decision to undergo spinal surgery last year for chronic back pain, he became a proponent of the procedure that changed his life for the better.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department, Louisville reach deal after probe prompted by Breonna Taylor killing
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — The U.S. Justice Department and the city of Louisville have reached an agreement to reform the city’s police force after an investigation prompted by the fatal police shooting of Breonna Taylor and police treatment of protesters, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fentanyl found inside dolphins in Gulf of Mexico
item.description: Traces of fentanyl were found inside bottlenose dolphins that were found dead in the Gulf of Mexico, raising concerns that the deadly drug may be affecting other sea life.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, Fox 5

item.title: Recall issued for 2.6 million travel mugs over burn risk
item.description: Stanley has announced a recall of approximately 2.6 million Switchback and Trigger Action travel mugs, citing a burn hazard. The recall, which was issued in cooperation with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: Many New Jersey 'drone' sightings are lawfully operated manned aircraft, White House says
item.description: White House national security spokesman John Kirby said there's no evidence that the New Jersey drone sightings are the work for foreign adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOJ: No undercover FBI agents were present for Jan. 6, countering conspiracy theories
item.description: The conclusion rejects theories that undercover FBI agents provoked the attack on the Capitol, which has since prompted charges for more than 1,500 figures involved in the riot.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: WarnerBros. Discovery announces major corporate restructure
item.description: Media conglomerate WarnerBros. Discovery (WBD) announced a major restructuring of its business on Thursday, splitting its linear and streaming arms into two separate companies as it eyes potential mergers and acquisitions in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: San Francisco names street for Associated Press photographer who captured the iconic Iwo Jima photo
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A photojournalist who captured one of the most enduring images of World War II — the U.S. Marines raising the flag on the Japanese island of Iwo Jima — will have a block in downtown San Francisco named for him Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House sends Biden bill adding judicial seats after veto threat
item.description: The House approved a bill Thursday that would create dozens of additional judicial seats over the next several years, sending it to President Biden’s desk after his administration levied a veto threat.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: Fire danger diminishes in Southern California even as crews continue to battle Malibu blaze
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — As weather improved in Southern California, firefighters found some success Wednesday battling a wind-driven blaze burning in steep, nearly inaccessible areas that forced thousands, including celebrities, from their homes in Malibu, fire officials said.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JAE C. HONG and GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin kayaker who faked his death and fled the country is charged after returning to US
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning and left his wife and three children for Eastern Europe willingly returned to the U.S. after four months and was charged Wednesday with obstructing an intense lake search for his body.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The man accused of killing the head of one of the biggest U.S. health insurance companies is fighting being sent to face charges in New York City, where the attack happened.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gun found on suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO matches shell casings at scene, police say
item.description: ALTOONA, Pa. (AP) — The gun found on the man charged with killing United Healthcare’s CEO matched shell casings found at the site of the shooting, New York City’s police commissioner said Wednesday as authorities scrutinized evidence and the suspect's experiences with the victim's industry.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Grave of famed abolitionist designated L.A. County historical landmark
item.description: The Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors unanimously voted Tuesday to designate the gravesite of abolitionist Owen Brown as a Los Angeles County Historical Landmark, marking the conclusion of a nearly yearlong process.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Wanted posters appear in NYC listing healthcare CEOs after shooting
item.description: Videos circulating on social media show wanted posters in New York City with photos of healthcare CEOs a week after the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in Midtown.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Amazing Kreskin, renowned mentalist and frequent late-night guest, dies at 89
item.description: The Amazing Kreskin, an entertainer and mentalist who rose to fame amid frequent appearances on TV talk shows like “The Tonight Show” and “The Mike Douglas Show,” has died, his family confirmed on social media. He was 89 years old.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Fentanyl found inside dead dolphins in Gulf of Mexico
item.description: Drug smugglers using open waterways to move products may be affecting the health of sea life in the Gulf of Mexico
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Albertsons gives up on Kroger merger, sues the grocery chain
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons in 2022 proposed what would be the largest grocery store merger in U.S. history. The companies said a merger would help them better compete with big retailers like Walmart, Costco and Amazon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione's fingerprints match water bottle found near shooting: sources
item.description: Fingerprints taken from Luigi Mangione in Pennsylvania matched those on a water bottle found near the scene of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's fatal shooting, sources said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Adorable reaction caught on camera as infant gets new glasses
item.description: An adorable reaction was caught on camera when an infant received a brand new pair of glasses that allowed him to see his parents clearly for the very first time.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Your daily cup of coffee could get more expensive because of climate change
item.description: Coffee lovers and café dwellers are likely to see the price of a cup of joe increase soon, experts warn.
item.dc:creator: Maya Eaglin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kayaker who faked his death, fled the US charged after returning to Wisconsin
item.description: Police had previously said they believed Borgwardt planned his Aug. 12 disappearance and tried to conceal his efforts to mislead investigations.
item.dc:creator: Indiana Schilz, Fox 5

item.title: Increased Christianity in schools opens the door to Satanic Temple education programs
item.description: The in-school offerings are in addition to the After School Satan Clubs, which launched in 2020 and have risen in popularity among parents seeking alternatives to religious clubs.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione was reported missing 2 weeks before CEO slain: police
item.description: The parents of Luigi Mangione, the man suspected of shooting and killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, told law enforcement that they hadn't heard from their son in months.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Nicole Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect intends to plead not guilty: lawyer
item.description: Alleged UnitedHealthcare CEO shooter Luigi Mangione was arrested at a Pennsylvania McDonald's on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah's household debt jumps over $1B in 3 months -- here's where we rank nationally
item.description: From Q2 to Q3 of 2024, Utah's total household debt increased by more than $1 billion — here's where Utah ranks compared to the rest of the country.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4
